informal: Prove that a group of order 312 has a normal Sylow $p$-subgroup for some prime $p$ dividing its order.
lean:
theorem Dummit_Foote_exercise_4_5_14 {G : Type*} [Group G] [Fintype G]
  (hG : card G = 312) :
  ∃ (p : ℕ) (P : Sylow p G), p.Prime ∧ (p ∣ card G) ∧ P.Normal
reasoning: The Lean signature asserts exactly what the informal statement claims: there exists a prime p dividing |G| = 312 and a Sylow p-subgroup P of G that is normal. The hypotheses (G a finite group of order 312) match, and the conclusion (existence of a normal Sylow p-subgroup for some prime p dividing the order) matches the informal claim. The conditions p.Prime and p ∣ card G are appropriate (and ensure we are talking about a genuine Sylow subgroup for a relevant prime). This is a faithful formalization.
